title: flow contracts (disallow flow a->b)
manager: Flow contracts — `disallow flow a -> b` — that forbid information from flowing between designated parts of a system, checked as taint-product reachability. This is the security and privacy primitive: assert that secret data can never reach a public output and have the checker prove it, which is exactly the kind of property that is easy to state, catastrophic to get wrong, and impossible to test exhaustively.
cs: `disallow flow a->b` = taint-product reachability; information-flow contracts; proves non-interference; a security/privacy checking primitive.
